exercises.title: Distribution Analysis (Histogram Bins)
exercises.description: Compute and display a text-based histogram for numerical data. Write `histogram(data, bins=5)` that divides the data range into equal-width bins and counts values in each bin. Print a formatted ASCII histogram. Test with salary data.
exercises.starterCode:
def histogram(data, bins=5, title=""):
    """
    Compute and print an ASCII histogram.
    bins: number of equal-width bins
    """
    min_val = min(data)
    max_val = max(data)
    bin_width = (max_val - min_val) / bins

    # TODO: Count values in each bin
    # Bin i covers: [min_val + i*bin_width, min_val + (i+1)*bin_width)
    # Last bin is inclusive on both ends
    counts = [0] * bins
    for value in data:
        # TODO: Compute which bin this value belongs to
        # bin_idx = min(int((value - min_val) / bin_width), bins - 1)
        pass

    max_count = max(counts)
    bar_scale = 20 / max_count if max_count > 0 else 1

    if title:
        print(title)
    print()

    for i in range(bins):
        low = min_val + i * bin_width
        high = low + bin_width
        bar_len = int(counts[i] * bar_scale)
        # TODO: Print: "$low-$high   |█████  count"
        pass
    print()

salaries = [
    45000, 52000, 48000, 61000, 55000, 59000, 72000, 67000, 63000,
    50000, 58000, 70000, 65000, 49000, 54000, 68000, 71000, 56000,
    47000, 66000
]

histogram(salaries, bins=5, title="Salary Distribution")
hints: For each bin, format the low and high bounds.

exercises.title: Box Plot Statistics
exercises.description: Compute and display box plot statistics (min, Q1, median, Q3, max, and outliers) for multiple groups. This is the numerical foundation of Seaborn's boxplot. Write `box_stats(data)` and a `print_box_plots(groups)` function that shows side-by-side box statistics.
exercises.starterCode:
def quartile(data, q):
    sorted_d = sorted(data)
    n = len(sorted_d)
    idx = q * (n - 1)
    lo, hi = int(idx), min(int(idx) + 1, n - 1)
    frac = idx - lo
    return sorted_d[lo] + frac * (sorted_d[hi] - sorted_d[lo])

def box_stats(data):
    """
    Compute box plot statistics.
    Returns dict: min, q1, median, q3, max, outliers, iqr, lower_fence, upper_fence
    """
    q1 = quartile(data, 0.25)
    median = quartile(data, 0.5)
    q3 = quartile(data, 0.75)
    iqr = q3 - q1
    # TODO: Compute lower_fence = q1 - 1.5*iqr, upper_fence = q3 + 1.5*iqr
    # TODO: Find outliers: values outside the fences
    # TODO: Return the stats dict
    pass

groups = {
    "Engineering": [85000, 92000, 78000, 95000, 110000, 88000, 72000, 125000, 91000, 84000],
    "Marketing":   [55000, 62000, 58000, 70000, 65000, 52000, 68000, 61000, 57000, 64000],
    "HR":           [48000, 52000, 56000, 50000, 60000, 45000, 54000, 49000, 51000, 55000],
}

print(f"{'Dept':<15} {'Min':>7} {'Q1':>7} {'Median':>8} {'Q3':>7} {'Max':>7} {'Outliers'}")
print("-" * 70)
for dept, salaries in groups.items():
    stats = box_stats(salaries)
    outlier_str = str(sorted(stats['outliers'])) if stats['outliers'] else "none"
    print(f"{dept:<15} {stats['min']:>7.0f} {stats['q1']:>7.0f} {stats['median']:>8.0f} "
          f"{stats['q3']:>7.0f} {stats['max']:>7.0f}  {outlier_str}")
hints: lower_fence = q1 - 1.5 * iqr; upper_fence = q3 + 1.5 * iqr
outliers = [x for x in data if x < lower_fence or x > upper_fence]
Return a dict with all computed values.
min and max use Python's built-in min() and max().

exercises.title: Correlation Matrix
exercises.description: Compute a correlation matrix between numeric variables (the foundation of Seaborn heatmaps). Write `correlation_matrix(records, columns)` that computes Pearson correlation between all column pairs. Print the matrix as a formatted table with values rounded to 2 decimal places.
exercises.starterCode:
def mean(values):
    return sum(values) / len(values)

def pearson_correlation(x, y):
    """
    Compute Pearson correlation coefficient between two equal-length lists.
    r = sum((xi - mean_x)(yi - mean_y)) / sqrt(sum((xi-mean_x)^2) * sum((yi-mean_y)^2))
    """
    n = len(x)
    mx, my = mean(x), mean(y)

    # TODO: Compute denom_y = sqrt(sum of (yi - my)^2)

data = [
    {"height": 170, "weight": 65, "age": 25, "score": 82},
    {"height": 165, "weight": 60, "age": 30, "score": 78},
    {"height": 180, "weight": 80, "age": 28, "score": 85},
    {"height": 175, "weight": 72, "age": 35, "score": 88},
    {"height": 160, "weight": 55, "age": 22, "score": 75},
    {"height": 172, "weight": 68, "age": 29, "score": 80},
    {"height": 168, "weight": 63, "age": 32, "score": 84},
    {"height": 185, "weight": 85, "age": 27, "score": 87},
]

warmup.title: Quick Recall: Quartile Calculation
warmup.starterCode:
data = sorted([3, 7, 8, 5, 12, 14, 21, 13, 18])
n = len(data)
print(data)
# Simple Q1 and Q3 using lower half and upper half medians
lower = data[:n//2]
upper = data[n//2+1:]
print(f"Q1 ~ {lower[len(lower)//2]}")
print(f"Q3 ~ {upper[len(upper)//2]}")
